steps.title: Track the narrator's viewpoint
steps.content: The narrator says her original country and adopted country differ widely, yet she sincerely loves both. She imagines herself looking at "two separate worlds" with "measuring eyes" and says she tried to explain unfamiliar American customs through Japanese standards.

steps.title: Choose cultural interpretation
steps.content: Choice B invents companions and sharing customs, neither of which appears in the passage. Choice C invents hesitation to ask questions, even though the narrator says people around her did not know the origins of customs. Choice D takes the "cloud in space" image as literal physical distance, but the image describes cultural perspective. The passage mainly presents the narrator observing and trying to understand differences between two cultures she embraces. The answer is A.
